district.name: Lakewood Unified School District
district.motto: Every Student, Every Day, All the Way

assessment.strengths: Strong teacher quality, improving graduation rates, active community partnerships, robust career technical education programs
assessment.challenges: Persistent achievement gaps for students of color and low-income students, chronic absenteeism in middle schools, limited advanced program access for underrepresented students
assessment.dataHighlights: Graduation rate: 87% (target: 95%). Four-year algebra proficiency: 62% (target: 80%). Chronic absenteeism: 12% (target: 5%). English learner reclassification: 45%.
assessment.equityFocus: Hispanic/Latino students, African American students, students from low-income backgrounds, students with disabilities, English learners

vision.statement: Every student in Lakewood Unified achieves academic excellence, develops critical thinking and collaboration skills, and graduates prepared for college, career, and community leadership.

mission.statement: We provide rigorous, culturally responsive instruction, equitable access to advanced programs, and comprehensive support to ensure every student reaches their highest potential and is prepared for success beyond high school.

coreValues.name: Equity
coreValues.definition: All students deserve fair access to opportunities and resources.
coreValues.fullDescription: Equity is at the heart of our work. We believe that zip code, income, race, or disability should never determine a student's access to rigorous, high-quality education.

coreValues.name: Excellence
coreValues.definition: We strive for the highest quality in everything we do.
coreValues.fullDescription: Excellence means pursuing the highest standards in our teaching, learning, and operations.

coreValues.name: Collaboration
coreValues.definition: We work together as a united team to achieve shared goals.
coreValues.fullDescription: Collaboration strengthens our work. We value diverse perspectives and leverage collective expertise.

coreValues.name: Student-Centered
coreValues.definition: We make decisions based on what is best for students.
coreValues.fullDescription: Student-centered means students are at the heart of everything we do.

coreValues.name: Growth Mindset
coreValues.definition: We believe all students and staff can grow and improve.
coreValues.fullDescription: Growth mindset means we see challenges as opportunities to learn.

selectedCompetencies.name: Critical Thinking
selectedCompetencies.description: Analyzes complex problems and develops evidence-based solutions.

selectedCompetencies.name: Lifelong Learning
selectedCompetencies.description: Maintains curiosity and love of learning.

foundations.name: Teaching Excellence
foundations.description: Coherent, rigorous, standards-aligned curriculum and evidence-based instruction.

foundations.name: Student Support Systems
foundations.description: Comprehensive systems to support students academically, behaviorally, and socially.

foundations.name: Professional Learning
foundations.description: Ongoing professional learning and collaboration focused on improvement.

foundations.name: Data and Improvement
foundations.description: Data systems and processes to drive decision-making and improvement.

goals.title: Increase Reading Proficiency
goals.description: Improve reading achievement and proficiency in all grade levels through evidence-based instruction.

measures.name: Percentage of students meeting reading standards
Reading growth from fall to spring

goals.title: Close Achievement Gaps
goals.description: Reduce disparities in achievement across racial and ethnic groups through targeted support and equitable practices.

initiatives.title: Implement balanced literacy framework K-5
initiatives.description: Adopt and implement a comprehensive balanced literacy approach with guided reading, independent reading, and writing.
initiatives.successIndicator: 100% of elementary teachers implement framework with fidelity
